frameworks.name: inter-subject correlation (ISC) analysis
frameworks.one_line: shared time-locked fMRI response across viewers indexes naturalistic stimulus processing

frameworks.name: naturalistic stimulus neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: free viewing of movies replaces standard controlled task paradigms

core_question: Do schizophrenia patients show reduced inter-subject neural synchronization during naturalistic humor processing compared to healthy controls?
key_assumptions: ISC indexes stimulus-locked shared cortical processing
comedy clips evoke comparable humor-related responses across viewers
patient and control groups are matchable on age and sex
key_findings: patients showed significant ISC in bilateral lateral occipital, superior frontal, left supramarginal, and right lateral orbitofrontal cortices
patients had lower ISC than controls in left superior temporal sulcus, bilateral supramarginal, and bilateral inferior parietal cortices
higher PANSS total severity correlated with lower ISC in middle frontal and middle temporal regions
interpretations: schizophrenia involves reduced synchronization of higher-order social and language cortex during naturalistic stimulus processing
clinical symptom severity tracks the magnitude of desynchronization in associative cortex
